title: Quiz: Quantum Derivative Pricing
description: Test your understanding of quantum algorithms for derivative pricing

questions.question: What speedup does quantum amplitude estimation provide over classical Monte Carlo?
questions.explanation: Quantum amplitude estimation achieves O(1/ε) samples vs O(1/ε²) classical samples for error ε, providing a quadratic speedup in the number of oracle calls needed.

questions.question: How is the asset price distribution encoded in a quantum state?
questions.explanation: The quantum state |ψ⟩ = Σ √p_i |i⟩ encodes probabilities as squared amplitudes. Each basis state represents a price level, with amplitude √p_i so that measuring gives probability p_i.

questions.question: For which type of options is quantum Monte Carlo most beneficial?

options.text: European vanilla options with analytical solutions

questions.explanation: Quantum Monte Carlo is most beneficial for path-dependent options (Asian, barrier, lookback) where classical pricing requires many simulation paths. The quadratic speedup significantly reduces computation time.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of variational quantum algorithms?

options.text: They work on noisy near-term devices

questions.explanation: Variational quantum algorithms use shallow parameterized circuits that can tolerate noise on near-term quantum computers. The hybrid quantum-classical approach optimizes parameters classically.

questions.question: Quantum amplitude estimation can estimate expected values with quadratically fewer samples than classical methods.
questions.explanation: True. Quantum amplitude estimation achieves O(1/ε) convergence vs O(1/ε²) classical, meaning it needs quadratically fewer samples to achieve the same error ε in estimating expected values.

questions.question: What classical technique does quantum pricing primarily speed up?
questions.explanation: Quantum pricing primarily speeds up Monte Carlo simulation by achieving faster convergence in estimating expected values. Options with analytical formulas like Black-Scholes don't benefit as much.

questions.question: What is the parameter shift rule used for in variational quantum circuits?
questions.explanation: The parameter shift rule allows computing exact gradients of parameterized quantum circuits by evaluating the circuit at shifted parameter values. This enables gradient-based optimization of variational circuits.

questions.question: Which are current challenges for quantum derivative pricing? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Current challenges include limited qubit counts, hardware noise affecting results, and the need for error mitigation techniques. The algorithms themselves are known - it's implementation on real hardware that's challenging.
